utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all 5 of these sections are present. Each section includes detailed information — coverage details and cost values where appropriate. Health coverage includes physical and mental health and costs; property coverage includes home/contents and costs; travel coverage includes coverage and cost (even if cost is zero, it is specified); additional_services lists enhancements with no missing cost detail requirement (these are specified as included services); total_budget includes monthly and annual totals. Therefore, all 5 required items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The AI assistant selected three main components in the final coverage plan: (1) 'LifeFirst Standard Combo' for health coverage, (2) 'LifeFirst Standard Combo Property' for property coverage, and (3) 'LifeFirst Standard Combo Travel' for travel coverage. The 'LifeFirst Standard Combo' appears to be a bundled version of 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' for both physical and mental health. In the ground-truth data, 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' has a rating of 9 for both physical and mental health. The property portion corresponds to 'LifeFirst Standard Coverage', which also has a rating of 9. The travel coverage corresponds to 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under mobility coverage, which has a rating of 9. Therefore, all three LifeFirst-derived components map to a rating of 9 each. The additional services in the plan (concierge, priority claims, direct billing, 24/7 support, multilingual support) do not have explicit individual ratings in the ground-truth data, but they are supplementary features of the main plans rather than standalone rated options.
